title: arrhenatherum elatius subsp. nebrodense
intro: Arrhenatherum elatius subsp. nebrodense, also known as Mountain Melick or Alpine Melick, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the grass family Poaceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, with reported health benefits including reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, Arrhenatherum elatius subsp. nebrodense is often used as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of conditions, from minor skin irritations to more serious health issues.
